SpTU 5, 247 [unidentified incantation or ritual][via cams/gkab]

Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

[...] x x [...]

(o ii 1') [...] ... [...]

o ii 2'2'

⸢2⸣ NU-MEŠ ša₂ TAG? RI? [x] na-šu x x x

(o ii 2') they bear 2 figures of ... [...] ...

o ii 3'3'

⸢ana⸣ E₂ x TA URU₂ E₂? GIM MIN<(te-te-mir)>

(o ii 3') to the house you bury ... like the house.1

o ii 4'4'

2 NU-MEŠ dlaḫ-mu ina TE? UŠ? x x ⸢TUR₃?  ; ZA₃⸣ u ⸢GUB₃⸣ MIN<(te-te-mir)>

(o ii 4') On the right and the left you bury two figures of Lahmu at the side of ... .

o ii 5'5'

2 NU-MEŠ x x x x x [x x]  ; ⸢ZA₃⸣ u ⸢GUB₃⸣ MIN<(te-te-mir)>

(o ii 5') On the right and the left you bury two figures ... [...].

o ii 6'6'

2 NU-MEŠ UR.IDIM ina TE ⸢KA₂ E₂ TU₁₅⸣-[2]  ; ⸢ZA₃⸣ [x] ⸢GUB₃⸣ MIN<(te-te-mir)>

(o ii 6') On the right and the left you bury two figures of the Wild Dog at the side of the door of the house to the north.

o ii 7'7'

2 NU-MEŠ MUŠ.ŠA₃.⸢TUR ina? TE?⸣ x x E₂ TU₁₅-⸢3  ; ZA₃⸣ u ⸢GUB₃⸣ MIN<(te-te-mir)>

(o ii 7') On the right and the left you bury two figures of a poisonous snake at the side of the ... of the house to the east.

o ii 8'8'

2 NU-MEŠ MUŠ.ḪUŠ ina TE x E₂ TU₁₅-4  ; ZA₃ u GUB₃ MIN<(te-te-mir)>

(o ii 8') On the right and the left you bury two figures of a serpent dragon at the side of the ... of the house to the west.

Column iii
(start of column missing)
o iii 1'o 1'

[...]-⸢MEŠ?⸣ [...]

(o iii 1') [...] ... [...]

o iii 2'2'

ana dLAMMA ina E₂ LU₂ [...]

(o iii 2') for [...] the protective goddess in a man's house

o iii 3'3'

gišbi-nu u₂IN₆.UŠ₂ gišGIŠIMMAR.TUR x [x (x)]

(o iii 3') tamarisk, maštakal-plant, a palm-shoot ... [...]

o iii 4'4'

gišNAM.TAR NITA₂ gišŠU.TAG u₂x [x (x)]

(o iii 4') male mandragora, ...-wood, ...-plant [...]

o iii 5'5'

⸢U₂⸣.DILIu₂ U₂ DU₃!(NI)A.BI u₂LU₂.U₁₈.⸢LU⸣ [x (x)]

(o iii 5') Asa foetida, all types of plant, amīlānu-plant [...]

o iii 6'6'

u₂eli-kul-la gišGAN.U₅ NAGA.⸢SI⸣ [x (x)]

(o iii 6') elkulla-plant, ...-wood, glasswort [...]

o iii 7'7'

GAZIsar u₂ak-tam KA tam-ti₃ [x (x)]

(o iii 7') mustard, aktam-plant, algae [...]

o iii 8'8'

ESIR.U₄.A PIŠ₁₀!(KI)dID₂ UḪ₂.d⸢ID₂⸣ [x (x)]

(o iii 8') pitch, kibrītu-sulphur, ruʾtītu-sulphur [...]

o iii 9'9'

AN.BAR ZABAR KU₃.BABBAR KU₃.SI₂₂ na₄⸢GUG⸣ [x (x)]

(o iii 9') iron, bronze, silver, gold, carnelian [...]

o iii 10'10'

na₄ZA.GIN₃ na₄NIR₂ na₄MUŠ.GIR₂ A ⸢PU₂⸣

(o iii 10') lapis lazuli, chalcedony, serpentine, water from the well at the temple of Marduk, you set up in a holy water vessel.

o iii 11'11'

ša₂ E₂ d⸢AMAR.UTU⸣ ina [dug]A.GUB₂.BA ⸢GUB⸣an

o iii 12'12'

ina ⸢KISAL?⸣ x IR? ⸢KU?⸣ DU-ma dugA.[DA.GUR₅ x (x)]

(o iii 12') ... in the courtyard ... and [...] a libation vessel.

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

ina ŠU ZA₃-šu₂ GIR₂ [...]

(r i 1) In his right hand [...] a sword (made) of [...].

r i 22

ina ŠU GUB₃-šu₂ ḫa-ṣi-in ⸢URUDU⸣ [...]

(r i 2) In his left hand [...] an axe (made) of copper.

r i 33

ina KA₂ AN.AŠ.AM₃ PU₂ BADte-ma x [...]

(r i 3) At the outer door you open a cistern and they stand ... [...]. ... [...] honey, milk, wine, oil.

r i 44

GUB?zu LAL₃ GA gišGEŠTIN I₃.GIŠ x [...]

r i 55

UR₅.GIM DU₁₁.[GA]

(r i 5) You speak as follows:

r i 66

EN₂ ⸢at-ta NU⸣ sa-kip lem-nu u ⸢a⸣-[a-bi]

(r i 6) Incantation: 'You are the figure that pushes away the foe and the enemy.

r i 77

mi-qit ANe dan-dan-nu

(r i 7) 'Fallen piece of heaven, all-powerful one,

r i 88

git₂-ma-lu ina DINGIR-MEŠ gaš-ru ša₂ me₂-lam-[mu] [x x]

(r i 8) 'perfect one under the gods, strong one who is endowed with [...] fearsome radiance, from the start [...] before Ea.

r i 99

ra-mu-u₂ ana IGI de₂-a ul-la-an [x x]

r i 1010

15-ka u 2.30-ka u₂-ṣur la te-⸢eg⸣-[gi]

(r i 10) 'Protect to your right and to your left. You must not be negligent.

r i 1111

lu-u₂ AN.TA.ŠUB.BA lu-u₂ ŠU ⸢GIDIM⸣.[MA]

(r i 11) 'Whether falling sickness or the hand of a ghost

r i 1212

lu-u₂ mim-ma lem-nu ša₂ ina E₂-ia₅ GARnu

(r i 12) 'or anything evil that has settled on my house

r i 1313

up-ta-na-al-laḫ-an-[ni]

(r i 13) 'perpetually terrifies me,

r i 1414

ina qi₂-bit de₂-a u dAMAR.UTU ia-a-[ši]

(r i 14) 'at the command of Ea and Marduk may it not approach me and my house. May it not proceed toward me.

r i 1515

u E₂-ia₅ a-a TEa a-a DIM₄-an-[ni]

r i 1616

a-a iq-ri-ba a-a KUR-an-ni li-bir ⸢ID₂⸣

(r i 16) 'May it not come near me. May it not reach me. (Instead) may it go across the watercourse.

r i 1717

lib-bal-kit KURa li-is-se ŠAR₂ DANNA ina ⸢SU⸣-[ia₅]

(r i 17) 'May it cross over the mountain. May it withdraw three thousand, six hundred leagues from my body.'

r i 1818

7-šu₂ u 7-šu₂ MAŠ.MAŠ DU₁₁.GA-ma GIR₃ ḪULti₃ KU₅⸢at⸣

(r i 18) The incantation priest speaks 7 times and 7 times and the foot of evil is blocked.

r i 1919

EN₂ gu₂-gal al-zi-ib al-ki-bi ⸢al⸣-[...]

(r i 19) Incantation: 'Foremost one, ... [...]

r i 2020

al-li-bi al-zi-⸢ib⸣ [...]

(r i 20) '... [...]

r i 2121

al-ḪU-ḪU na-an-DI? [...]

(r i 21) '... [...]

r i 2222

an ki-a a₂ ḫu-mu-ra-be₂ al-gi-⸢bad?⸣

(r i 22) 'throughout heaven and earth he exercises power ...

r i 2323

IŠ ḫu-mu-ra-am-maḫ-di IŠ [...]

(r i 23) '... he is exalted ... [...].'

r i 2424

KA.INIM.MA GIR₃ ḪULti₃ ina E₂ LU₂ KU₅at

(r i 24) Wording (that) the foot of evil be blocked from a man's house.

r i 2525

IM PA₅ gišKIRI₆ TIqe₂ ina A-MEŠ qid-da-⸢ti⸣

(r i 25) You take clay from a ditch in an orchard. In water (flowing) downstream [...] ... 2 figures ... [...]

r i 2626

[x x] A NU 2 NU-MEŠ ana ⸢giš?⸣x ⸢MI AḪ⸣ [...]

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

⸢DU₃.DU₃-BI⸣ x x x x x x PIŠ₁₀!(KI)dID₂

(r ii 1) Its ritual: You mix together ..., kibrītu-sulphur, ruʾtītu-sulphur, magnetite (and) iron.

r ii 22

UḪ₂.dID₂ na₄KA.GI.NA.DAB.BA AN.BAR

r ii 33

1niš ḪI.ḪI 2 NU-MEŠ ina ZA₃ KA₂ u GUB₃ KA₂

(r ii 3) You draw 2 figures on the right of the door and on the left of the door. You draw one on top of the door.

r ii 44

tu-ṣar!(KU-)šu₂-nu-tu₂ 1en ina UGU gišIG ḪURir

r ii 55

ina ZA₃ EN te-eṣ-ṣi-ru UR₅.GIM DU₁₁.GA

(r ii 5) While you draw on the right, you speak as follows:

r ii 66

na-ad-ru gap-šu sa-ak-pa <i₃>-li₂

(r ii 6) 'Wild, swollen, rejected by the god,

r ii 77

kip-pu tar-ṣu da-an pa-ni

(r ii 7) 'spread trap, firm in intention

r ii 88

la a-di-ru šag-gi-šu₂

(r ii 8) 'fearless, murderous,

r ii 99

u₄-mu da-i-ku u₂-tuk-ku lem-nu

(r ii 9) 'killing storm-demon, evil demon.'

r ii 1010

ina GUB₃ EN te-eṣ-ṣi-ru kam DU₁₁.GA

(r ii 10) While you draw on the left, you speak as follows:

r ii 1111

mu-⸢um⸣-me ki-šad ḪULti₃ ur-ru-ḫu la-pi-it UŠ₂ : 1

(r ii 11) 'Creator of the neck of evil, hustler, smearing with blood,

r ii 1212

mu-un-na-ar-bu la-as-si-mu

(r ii 12) 'fleet-footed, swift,

r ii 1313

la mu-ki-il kiš-⸢šat⸣ ILLAT-šu₂

(r ii 13) 'not restraining his entire band,

r ii 1414

GIR₃ ḪULti₃ KU₅us

(r ii 14) 'keep away the foot of evil.'

r ii 1515

ina gišIG EN te-eṣ-ṣi-ru kam DU₁₁.GA

(r ii 15) While you draw on the door, you speak as follows:

r ii 1616

⸢ez⸣-ze-ta šam-ra-ta gap-ša₂-ta gaṣ-ṣa-ta : 2

(r ii 16) 'You are angry, you are furious, you are swollen, you are cruel,

r ii 1717

[na]-ad-ra-ta gap-ša₂-na-ta na-ad-ra-na-ta : 3

(r ii 17) 'you are wild, you are particularly swollen, you are particularly wild,

r ii 1818

pa-⸢aš₂⸣-<qa>-ta šu?-ut ḪUL-ME ša₂ la de₂-a

(r ii 18) 'you are difficult, you are the one who is evil. Without Ea ... can calm you? (Only) Ea can calm you.'

r ii 1919

x ⸢li?⸣-ni-iḫ-⸢ku⸣-nu-ši de₂-a li-ni-iḫ-ku-nu-ši : 4

r ii 2020

GIM ⸢qin⸣-na-za te-te-ri-šu₂ PAD gišDALA₂ gišGIŠIMMAR

(r ii 20) When you have requested a whip, you take the ... of palm tree spikes. You bury (it) to the right and left of the door.

r ii 2121

TIqe₂ ZA₃ u GUB₃ ša₂ KA₂ te-te-mir

r ii 2222

EN₂ E₂.NU.RU na₄ka-gin₆-na dab-ba ⸢keš₂-keš₂⸣

(r ii 22) Enuru-incantation: 'Binding magnetite,

r ii 2323

dx [x x] IGI ⸢IN?⸣ ŠE? ⸢KA?⸣ x NI ⸢SAG?⸣ [...]-ga-a-ni : 5

(r ii 23) '... [...] ... [...] ...,

r ii 2424

ka ⸢ḫulu⸣-[ŋal₂ x x] ⸢ḫe₂⸣-em-ta-e₃

(r ii 24) 'may the evil mouth [...] leave,

r ii 2525

eme ⸢ḫulu⸣-[ŋal₂ x x] ⸢ḫe₂⸣-em-ta-e₃

(r ii 25) 'may the evil tongue [...] leave,

r ii 2626

niŋ₂-⸢nam ḫulu⸣-[ŋal₂ x x] ⸢ḫe₂⸣-em-ta-e₃

(r ii 26) 'may anything evil [...] leave.'

r ii 2727

[...] x [x x]

(r ii 27) [...] ... [...]
